_comment: Compact plan authored by the LLM from ONE pass over the source document. Everything else is generated from this file.

source.title: <short title>
source.year: <preferred patent year if different from publication year>

<flow_key>.canonical_flow_key: <optional: another flow_key in this map to emit instead (e.g. anhydrous form)>

processes.technology: <LCI source text for UI 处理、标准、路线: route, operation/equipment, technical parameters, standards/basis; quote or closely paraphrase the patent/SOP where possible>
processes.comment: <audit summary: source, measured/calculated/estimated basis, missing-data limits>
processes.reference_output_flow: <flow_key that is the reference product OUT>

inputs.comment: <optional audit note carried into the ILCD exchange comment>

outputs.comment: <optional audit note carried into the ILCD exchange comment>

_rules: Use the SAME flow_key across processes to create edges (upstream OUTPUT key == downstream INPUT key).
Every process must have exactly one output whose flow equals reference_output_flow; that is the reference exchange.
Amounts are per 1 unit of the process's reference output.

Fill plan-level fields before materialization: source.id/title/assignee when available, best patent dates/year, goal.name/functional_unit/boundary, geography, reference_year, and every referenced flow name_en/name_zh when available/unit.

When starting from a patent metadata CSV row plus patent text, put bibliographic/triage metadata in source and source.extra_metadata, but derive technology, amounts, calculation notes, and source_quote/source_ref from the patent/SOP text.
Fill all review-required process fields: key, step_id, name_en, name_zh when available, classification, scale, technology, comment, black_box, pure_oxygen, reference_output_flow, inputs, and outputs.

processes[].technology maps to processInformation.technology.technologyDescriptionAndIncludedProcesses, the UI 处理、标准、路线 field. Put the LCI source text and technical parameters there: route sequence, equipment/operation, temperatures, times, concentrations, pH, ratios, pressure, atmosphere, flow rates, yields, washing/drying/calcination conditions, and standards or engineering basis.

derivation=Measured: the source text gives this exact quantity. derivation=Calculated: derived from source-given molar ratios/formulas/recipe fractions — MUST supply calc_note. derivation=Estimated: defaulted from engineering models (electricity/water/O2/waste should come from estimate-utilities.mjs).

Every exchange must include flow, amount, and derivation. Add source_ref or source_quote for source-supported Measured/Calculated values; add formula_ref and source_ref for Estimated values from an estimator or auxiliary source.
For Estimated electricity/water/O2/waste exchanges, copy the estimator formula_ref and any source_ref into the exchange; materialize-from-plan carries those fields into ILCD generalComment.

Black-box is last resort: set black_box=true only when critical material, product, or operation data needed for a defensible inventory are missing after trying Measured, Calculated, and Estimated derivations. Never mark a whole patent route black-box just because some exchanges are missing.

If a process is black_box=true because critical data are missing, every flow used by that black-box process must use unit=item and every exchange amount must be 1. Downstream processes consuming an item-unit flow inherit that item-based treatment.
pure_oxygen=true is required to declare any O2 input (flow key containing 'o2' or 'oxygen'). Only set it when the source explicitly names a pure-O2 atmosphere.

Hydrate and valence name variants are surfaced as flow-resolution review candidates, not auto-converted. After review, canonical_flow_key lets an audited flow (e.g. 'coso4_7h2o' hydrate) emit as another flow (e.g. 'coso4' anhydrous) with amount multiplied by conversion_factor (e.g. 154.99/281.10 ≈ 0.551). Both flows must be declared in this flows{} map; the canonical one is the form that actually enters the ILCD dataset.
Omit _comment and _rules in the actual plan; they are illustrative only.
